description: Girsanov / change-of-measure / Black-Scholes finance theorems from Saporito Ch 9

theorems.name: Novikov's Condition
theorems.description: Theorem 9.1.7: If E[exp((1/2) ∫₀ᵀ θ_s² ds)] < ∞, then the Doleans-Dade exponential Z_t = exp(∫₀ᵗ θ_s dB_s - (1/2) ∫₀ᵗ θ_s² ds) is a true martingale on [0,T]

code.lean:
import Mathlib

open MeasureTheory ProbabilityTheory

/-- Novikov-condition specification: a progressively measurable integrand θ
    on `[0, T]` with `E[exp((1/2) ∫₀ᵀ θ_s² ds)] < ∞` and a witness that the
    Doléans–Dade exponential `Z_t = exp(∫₀ᵗ θ_s dB_s − (1/2) ∫₀ᵗ θ_s² ds)`
    is a true martingale on `[0, T]` (Theorem 9.1.7). -/
structure NovikovCondition {Ω : Type*} [m0 : MeasurableSpace Ω]
    (μ : Measure Ω) [IsProbabilityMeasure μ] (𝓕 : Filtration ℝ m0)
    (T : ℝ) (Z : ℝ → Ω → ℝ) : Prop where
  T_pos : 0 < T
  /-- Stand-in for the Novikov hypothesis: a uniform L¹ bound on `Z`. The
      genuine condition `E[exp(½∫₀ᵀ θ² ds)] < ∞` needs the stochastic integral
      `∫ θ dB` (no `θ` or `B` appears in this structure) and is not encoded. -/
  novikov_finite : ∃ R : ℝ, ∀ t ∈ Set.Icc (0 : ℝ) T,
    eLpNorm (Z t) 1 μ ≤ ENNReal.ofReal R
  /-- Theorem 9.1.7: `Z` is a martingale on `[0, T]`. -/
  is_martingale : Martingale Z 𝓕 μ

/-- Theorem 9.1.7 (Novikov's condition): under the Novikov integrability
    condition, the Doléans–Dade exponential `Z` is a true martingale on `[0, T]`. -/
theorem NovikovCondition.doleansDade_is_martingale
    {Ω : Type*} [m0 : MeasurableSpace Ω] {μ : Measure Ω} [IsProbabilityMeasure μ]
    {𝓕 : Filtration ℝ m0} {T : ℝ} {Z : ℝ → Ω → ℝ}
    (h : NovikovCondition μ 𝓕 T Z) :
    Martingale Z 𝓕 μ :=
  h.is_martingale

metadata.formalization_scope: Lean specification (structure spec): the structure carries `T_pos`, a uniform L¹ bound on `Z` standing in for the Novikov integrability hypothesis — the genuine condition E[exp(½∫₀ᵀ θ² ds)] < ∞ requires the stochastic integral ∫θ dB, and no θ or B appears in the structure — and the conclusion `Martingale Z 𝓕 μ` as a field; the theorem reads off the conclusion via a structural projection. This is statement-level encoding, not a derivation. Marked `reduced_core` to remain honest: a real proof requires the Itô-integral / Doléans–Dade machinery currently outside Mathlib at the pinned toolchain.

theorems.name: Girsanov: Drifted BM under Equivalent Measure
theorems.description: Theorem 9.1.8 (1st version): For bounded θ, B^θ_t = B_t - ∫₀ᵗ θ_s ds is a Brownian motion under the measure dP^θ/dP = Z_T

code.lean:
import Mathlib

open MeasureTheory ProbabilityTheory

/-- Girsanov-theorem specification (1st version): a bounded drift `θ`, a
    P-Brownian motion `B`, the Doléans–Dade exponential `Z_T`, and the equivalent
    probability measure `Q = Z_T · P` under which the drift-corrected process
    `B^θ_t = B_t − ∫₀ᵗ θ_s ds` is a Brownian motion (Theorem 9.1.8). -/
structure GirsanovDriftBM {Ω : Type*} [MeasurableSpace Ω]
    (P : Measure Ω) [IsProbabilityMeasure P] (T : ℝ) (B Bθ : ℝ → Ω → ℝ)
    (θ : ℝ → Ω → ℝ) (Z_T : Ω → ℝ) where
  T_pos : 0 < T
  /-- B is a P-Brownian motion (zero start, Gaussian indep increments). -/
  zero_start : ∀ᵐ ω ∂P, B 0 ω = 0
  gaussian_increments : ∀ ⦃s t : ℝ⦄, s ≤ t →
    ∃ v : NNReal, (v : ℝ) = t - s ∧
      Measure.map (fun ω => B t ω - B s ω) P = gaussianReal 0 v
  independent_increments : ∀ ⦃s t u v : ℝ⦄, s ≤ t → t ≤ u → u ≤ v →
    IndepFun (fun ω => B t ω - B s ω) (fun ω => B v ω - B u ω) P
  /-- θ is uniformly bounded. -/
  theta_bounded : ∃ K : ℝ, ∀ t ω, |θ t ω| ≤ K
  /-- Definition of the drift-corrected process Bθ. -/
  Bθ_def : ∀ t ω, Bθ t ω = B t ω - ∫ s in (0 : ℝ)..t, θ s ω
  /-- Z_T is positive a.s. (P) and has unit P-mean. -/
  Z_pos : ∀ᵐ ω ∂P, 0 < Z_T ω
  Z_mean_one : ∫ ω, Z_T ω ∂P = 1
  /-- Theorem 9.1.8: under the equivalent measure Q with density Z_T,
      the drift-corrected process Bθ has Gaussian increments of variance t − s
      independent across disjoint intervals — i.e., it is a Q-Brownian motion. -/
  Q : Measure Ω
  Q_isProbability : IsProbabilityMeasure Q
  Q_density : Q = P.withDensity (fun ω => ENNReal.ofReal (Z_T ω))
  Bθ_Q_brownian :
    (∀ᵐ ω ∂Q, Bθ 0 ω = 0) ∧
    (∀ ⦃s t : ℝ⦄, s ≤ t →
      ∃ v : NNReal, (v : ℝ) = t - s ∧
        Measure.map (fun ω => Bθ t ω - Bθ s ω) Q = gaussianReal 0 v) ∧
    (∀ ⦃s t u v : ℝ⦄, s ≤ t → t ≤ u → u ≤ v →
      IndepFun (fun ω => Bθ t ω - Bθ s ω) (fun ω => Bθ v ω - Bθ u ω) Q)

/-- Theorem 9.1.8 (Girsanov, 1st version): under the equivalent measure `Q` with
    density `Z_T`, the drift-corrected process `B^θ_t = B_t − ∫₀ᵗ θ_s ds` is a
    Brownian motion (zero start, Gaussian independent increments). -/
theorem GirsanovDriftBM.driftCorrected_is_QBrownian
    {Ω : Type*} [MeasurableSpace Ω] {P : Measure Ω} [IsProbabilityMeasure P]
    {T : ℝ} {B Bθ : ℝ → Ω → ℝ} {θ : ℝ → Ω → ℝ} {Z_T : Ω → ℝ}
    (h : GirsanovDriftBM P T B Bθ θ Z_T) :
    (∀ᵐ ω ∂h.Q, Bθ 0 ω = 0) ∧
    (∀ ⦃s t : ℝ⦄, s ≤ t →
      ∃ v : NNReal, (v : ℝ) = t - s ∧
        Measure.map (fun ω => Bθ t ω - Bθ s ω) h.Q = gaussianReal 0 v) ∧
    (∀ ⦃s t u v : ℝ⦄, s ≤ t → t ≤ u → u ≤ v →
      IndepFun (fun ω => Bθ t ω - Bθ s ω) (fun ω => Bθ v ω - Bθ u ω) h.Q) :=
  h.Bθ_Q_brownian

metadata.formalization_scope: Lean specification: the structure encodes the textbook hypotheses and the textbook conclusion as fields, so any inhabitant satisfies the textbook statement by construction; the theorem reads off the conclusion via a structural projection. This is statement-level encoding, not a derivation of the textbook conclusion from foundational primitives. Marked `reduced_core` to remain honest: a real proof requires the underlying process / measure-theoretic infrastructure currently outside Mathlib at the pinned toolchain. NOTE (2026-07-05): the constant-θ special case is now fully proved from primitives — MathFin.Btheta_isQBrownianMotion (corpus gir-const-theta-qbm, full) derives the complete Q-Brownian motion (zero start, Gaussian AND independent increments) for constant θ, on the existing tower without an adapted-integrand Itô formula. What keeps THIS general entry reduced_core is the ADAPTED (path-dependent) drift θ_s, whose Doléans–Dade exponential requires the adapted-integrand Itô formula / pathwise quadratic variation still absent from the tower.

theorems.name: Black-Scholes Call Pricing Formula
theorems.description: Black-Scholes call price (Ch 9.4): C(S,t) = S Φ(d_1) - K e^{-r(T-t)} Φ(d_2) where d_1 = (log(S/K) + (r + σ²/2)(T-t))/(σ √(T-t)) and d_2 = d_1 - σ √(T-t)

code.lean:
import MathFin.BlackScholes.Call

open MeasureTheory ProbabilityTheory Real
open scoped NNReal ENNReal
open MathFin

variable {Ω : Type*} {mΩ : MeasurableSpace Ω}

/-- Theorem (Saporito Ch 9.4) — Black-Scholes European call pricing formula.

For an asset whose risk-neutral log-return is Gaussian (the standard BS
hypothesis), the discounted expected payoff of the European call
`max(S_T - K, 0)` equals `S_0 · Φ(d_1) − K · e^{-rT} · Φ(d_2)`, where:
  d_1 = (log(S_0/K) + (r + σ²/2)T) / (σ√T)
  d_2 = d_1 − σ√T

Wraps `MathFin.bs_call_formula`, which derives this from primitives:
HasLaw transfer to standard normal, max → indicator on exercise region
`{z : S_T(z) > K} = Set.Ioi(-d_2)`, completing-the-square integral
`∫ z in Ioi(-d_2), exp(σ√T·z) · pdf(0,1,z) = exp(σ²T/2) · Φ(d_1)`, and
the BS martingale algebra `(r − σ²/2)T + σ²T/2 = rT`.

No upstream Itô calculus / Girsanov required; pure Gaussian integration.
Axioms-clean per `#print axioms`: depends only on `propext`,
`Classical.choice`, `Quot.sound`. -/
theorem black_scholes_call_formula
    {Q : Measure Ω} [IsProbabilityMeasure Q]
    {S_0 K r σ T : ℝ} {Z : Ω → ℝ}
    (h : BSCallHyp Q S_0 K r σ T Z) :
    ∫ ω, Real.exp (-r * T) * max (bsTerminal S_0 r σ T (Z ω) - K) 0 ∂Q
      = S_0 * Phi (bsd1 S_0 K r σ T) -
        K * Real.exp (-r * T) * Phi (bsd2 S_0 K r σ T) :=
  MathFin.bs_call_formula h

metadata.formalization_scope: Full formal proof of the Black-Scholes European call pricing formula. Wraps MathFin.bs_call_formula, which derives E_Q[e^{-rT} max(S_T - K, 0)] = S_0 Phi(d_1) - K e^{-rT} Phi(d_2) from primitives: HasLaw.integral_comp (transfer ∫_Q to ∫_(gaussianReal 0 1)), integral_gaussianReal_eq_integral_smul (convert to ∫ with gaussianPDFReal), max_payoff_eq_indicator + integral_indicator (restrict to {z > -d_2}), integral_exp_mul_gaussianPDFReal_Ioi (completing-the-square, ∫ exp(σ√T z) pdf(0,1,z) = exp(σ²T/2) Phi(d_1)), gaussianReal_Ioi_toReal (∫ pdf = Phi(d_2)), and the BS martingale algebra (r-σ²/2)T + σ²T/2 = rT. Lives at MathFin/BlackScholes/Call.lean (~340 lines, Phi infrastructure + completing-the-square + shifted-tail integral + main theorem). No upstream Itô calculus / Girsanov machinery required — pure Gaussian integration. Axioms-clean per #print axioms: depends only on propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound.

theorems.name: Continuous-Time First FTAP: Discounted GBM is a Martingale
theorems.description: Under the risk-neutral measure, the discounted geometric Brownian motion price e^{−rt}S_t is a martingale with respect to the Brownian filtration — the defining property of the equivalent martingale measure and the operational content of the first fundamental theorem of asset pricing in continuous time.

code.lean:
import MathFin.Foundations.ContinuousFTAP

open MeasureTheory ProbabilityTheory
open scoped NNReal

/-- Continuous-time first FTAP, martingale property: under the risk-neutral measure,
    the discounted Black–Scholes price `e^{−rt}·S_t`, with
    `S_t = S₀·exp((r − σ²/2)t + σX_t)` driven by a filtered pre-Brownian motion `X`,
    is a martingale w.r.t. the Brownian filtration — the defining property of the
    equivalent martingale measure. Re-export of `MathFin.discountedGBM_isMartingale`
    (`MathFin/Foundations/ContinuousFTAP.lean`: the discounted price IS `S₀` times
    the Wald exponential `exp(σX_t − σ²t/2)`, a martingale by
    `IsFilteredPreBrownian.waldExponential_isMartingale`). -/
theorem discounted_gbm_is_Q_martingale
    {Ω : Type*} {mΩ : MeasurableSpace Ω} {P : Measure Ω}
    {𝓕 : Filtration ℝ≥0 mΩ} {X : ℝ≥0 → Ω → ℝ}
    [IsFilteredPreBrownian X 𝓕 P] [IsFiniteMeasure P] (S_0 r σ : ℝ) :
    Martingale (fun (t : ℝ≥0) ω => Real.exp (-r * t) *
        (S_0 * Real.exp ((r - σ ^ 2 / 2) * t + σ * X t ω))) 𝓕 P :=
  MathFin.discountedGBM_isMartingale S_0 r σ

metadata.reference: Risk-neutral pricing / first FTAP, continuous time (Saporito Ch 9; Shreve II Ch 5)
metadata.formalization_scope: Full formal proof. The discounted price e^{−rt}·S_t = S₀·exp(σX_t − σ²t/2) is S₀ • (Wald exponential), and IsFilteredPreBrownian.waldExponential_isMartingale (MathFin/Foundations/BrownianMartingale.lean: pull-out + condExp_indep_eq + Gaussian MGF) plus Martingale.smul give the martingale property — no further stochastic-calculus machinery. Continuous-time analogue of the discrete binomial EMM (Binomial/SecondFTAP.lean). Lives in MathFin/Foundations/ContinuousFTAP.lean. Axioms-clean.

theorems.name: Bayes Change-of-Measure Martingale Engine
theorems.description: The abstract kernel of Girsanov's theorem: if the density process Z and the product Z·D are both P-martingales and Z_T ≥ 0, then D is a martingale on [0,T] under the tilted measure Q = withDensity(Z_T). No stochastic calculus — only conditional expectations (a Bayes pull-out and a martingale set-integral). The reusable engine consumed by the Black-Scholes Girsanov instantiation.

code.lean:
import MathFin.Foundations.ChangeOfMeasure

open MeasureTheory ProbabilityTheory
open scoped NNReal ENNReal

/-- **Bayes change-of-measure martingale engine** (Girsanov's abstract kernel). If `Z`
    and the product `Z · D` are both `P`-martingales, `D` is adapted, and `Z_T ≥ 0` is a
    density, then `D` is a martingale on `[0, T]` under `Q = P.withDensity Z_T`: for
    `s ≤ t ≤ T` and `A ∈ 𝓕_s` the `Q`-integrals of `D_t` and `D_s` over `A` agree. No
    stochastic calculus — only conditional expectations (a Bayes pull-out plus a
    martingale set-integral). Re-exports `MathFin.changeOfMeasure_setIntegral_eq`. -/
theorem change_of_measure_martingale
    {Ω : Type*} {mΩ : MeasurableSpace Ω} {P : Measure Ω} [IsFiniteMeasure P]
    {𝓕 : Filtration ℝ≥0 mΩ} [SigmaFiniteFiltration P 𝓕]
    {Z D : ℝ≥0 → Ω → ℝ} (T : ℝ≥0)
    (hZmeasT : Measurable (Z T)) (hZpos : ∀ ω, 0 ≤ Z T ω)
    (hDsm : ∀ u, StronglyMeasurable[𝓕 u] (D u))
    (hZ : Martingale Z 𝓕 P)
    (hZD : Martingale (fun t ω => Z t ω * D t ω) 𝓕 P)
    (hmix : ∀ u, u ≤ T → Integrable (fun ω => D u ω * Z T ω) P)
    {s t : ℝ≥0} (hst : s ≤ t) (htT : t ≤ T)
    {A : Set Ω} (hA : MeasurableSet[𝓕 s] A) :
    ∫ ω in A, D t ω ∂(P.withDensity (fun ω => ENNReal.ofReal (Z T ω)))
      = ∫ ω in A, D s ω ∂(P.withDensity (fun ω => ENNReal.ofReal (Z T ω))) :=
  MathFin.changeOfMeasure_setIntegral_eq T hZmeasT hZpos hDsm hZ hZD hmix hst htT hA

metadata.reference: Girsanov / abstract change of measure (Saporito Ch 9; Shreve II Ch 5.2)
metadata.formalization_scope: Full formal proof re-exported from MathFin/Foundations/ChangeOfMeasure.lean. For Q = P.withDensity(ENNReal.ofReal Z_T), the identity ∫_A D_t dQ = ∫_A Z_t D_t dP = ∫_A Z_s D_s dP = ∫_A D_s dQ. The outer equalities are the Bayes pull-out: for A ∈ 𝓕_s ⊆ 𝓕_u and D_u 𝓕_u-measurable, ∫_A D_u Z_T dP = ∫_A D_u·P[Z_T|𝓕_u] dP = ∫_A D_u Z_u dP by the Z-martingale property (condExp_mul_of_stronglyMeasurable_left + setIntegral_condExp); the middle is the Z·D martingale set-integral (Martingale.setIntegral_eq). withDensity conversion via setIntegral_withDensity_eq_setIntegral_toReal_smul. Axioms-clean: propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound.

theorems.name: Black-Scholes EMM via an Explicit Girsanov Change of Measure
theorems.description: The Black-Scholes risk-neutral measure constructed as a Girsanov density change of the physical measure. Under physical drift μ the discounted stock D_t = S_0·exp((μ−r−σ²/2)t + σX_t) is only a submartingale; tilting P by the Girsanov density exp(−θX_T − ½θ²T) with market price of risk θ = (μ−r)/σ turns it into a martingale on [0,T] under Q = withDensity(Z_T). Q is the equivalent martingale measure, exhibited as an explicit change of measure — retiring the Wald shortcut of the continuous FTAP. The martingale side of the I↔II (pricing↔Itô) seam; the distributional Girsanov (B^θ = B − ∫θ ds is Q-Brownian, gir-thm-9.1.8) needs an adapted-integrand Itô formula and remains open.

code.lean:
import MathFin.Foundations.Girsanov

open MeasureTheory ProbabilityTheory
open scoped NNReal ENNReal

/-- **Black–Scholes EMM as an explicit Girsanov change of measure.** With physical drift
    `μ`, rate `r`, volatility `σ ≠ 0`, and market price of risk `θ = (μ − r)/σ`, the
    discounted stock `D_t = S_0·exp((μ − r − σ²/2)t + σX_t)` is a martingale on `[0, T]`
    under the tilted measure `Q = P.withDensity(exp(−θX_T − ½θ²T))` — the risk-neutral
    measure constructed as a Girsanov density change, retiring the Wald shortcut. The
    proof feeds two Wald exponentials (`Z`, `Z·D`) to the change-of-measure engine, with
    mixed-time integrability of `D_u·Z_T` via AM–GM. Re-exports
    `MathFin.bs_discounted_isQMartingale`. -/
theorem bs_emm_via_girsanov
    {Ω : Type*} {mΩ : MeasurableSpace Ω} {P : Measure Ω} [IsProbabilityMeasure P]
    {𝓕 : Filtration ℝ≥0 mΩ} [SigmaFiniteFiltration P 𝓕]
    {X : ℝ≥0 → Ω → ℝ} [IsFilteredPreBrownian X 𝓕 P]
    (S_0 r μ σ : ℝ) (hσ : σ ≠ 0) (T : ℝ≥0)
    {s t : ℝ≥0} (hst : s ≤ t) (htT : t ≤ T)
    {A : Set Ω} (hA : MeasurableSet[𝓕 s] A) :
    ∫ ω in A, (S_0 * Real.exp ((μ - r - σ ^ 2 / 2) * (t : ℝ) + σ * X t ω))
        ∂(P.withDensity fun ω => ENNReal.ofReal
          (Real.exp (-((μ - r) / σ) * X T ω - ((μ - r) / σ) ^ 2 * (T : ℝ) / 2)))
      = ∫ ω in A, (S_0 * Real.exp ((μ - r - σ ^ 2 / 2) * (s : ℝ) + σ * X s ω))
        ∂(P.withDensity fun ω => ENNReal.ofReal
          (Real.exp (-((μ - r) / σ) * X T ω - ((μ - r) / σ) ^ 2 * (T : ℝ) / 2))) :=
  MathFin.bs_discounted_isQMartingale S_0 r μ σ hσ T hst htT hA

metadata.reference: Girsanov change of measure / risk-neutral pricing (Saporito Ch 9; Shreve II Ch 5.2-5.4)
metadata.formalization_scope: Full formal proof re-exported from MathFin/Foundations/Girsanov.lean. Instantiates the change-of-measure engine (changeOfMeasure_setIntegral_eq) with two Wald exponentials: the Girsanov density Z = exp(−θX − ½θ²·) = waldExponential(−θ) and the product Z·D = S_0·exp((σ−θ)X − ½(σ−θ)²·) = S_0·waldExponential(σ−θ), both P-martingales by IsFilteredPreBrownian.waldExponential_isMartingale (using μ−r = σθ). Adaptedness of D via continuous-comp of the adapted X_u; the one new estimate is the mixed-time integrability of D_u·Z_T, handled by AM-GM exp(σX_u)exp(−θX_T) ≤ exp(2σX_u)+exp(−2θX_T), each Gaussian-MGF-integrable (integrable_exp_mul_of_hasLaw). Does NOT claim the distributional Girsanov (drift-corrected process is Q-Brownian). Axioms-clean: propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound.

theorems.name: Martingale Representation Theorem
theorems.description: Corollary 9.3.4: Every square-integrable martingale M adapted to the BM filtration can be written M_t = M_0 + ∫₀ᵗ ϕ_s dB_s for some ϕ ∈ H²

code.lean:
import Mathlib

open MeasureTheory ProbabilityTheory

/-- Martingale-representation specification on the Brownian filtration: every
    square-integrable martingale `M` adapted to the BM filtration on `[0, T]`
    has an integrand `φ ∈ H²` and an initial value `M_0` with
    `M_t = M_0 + ∫₀ᵗ φ_s dB_s` (Corollary 9.3.4). -/
structure MartingaleRepresentation {Ω : Type*} [m0 : MeasurableSpace Ω]
    (μ : Measure Ω) [IsProbabilityMeasure μ]
    (𝓕 : Filtration ℝ m0) (B : ℝ → Ω → ℝ) (T : ℝ)
    (M : ℝ → Ω → ℝ) : Prop where
  T_pos : 0 < T
  /-- M is a martingale on [0, T]. -/
  is_martingale : Martingale M 𝓕 μ
  /-- M is square-integrable on [0, T]. -/
  M_squareIntegrable : ∃ R : ℝ, ∀ t ∈ Set.Icc (0 : ℝ) T,
    eLpNorm (M t) 2 μ ≤ ENNReal.ofReal R
  /-- Corollary 9.3.4: there exists an H²-integrand φ and an initial value M₀
      such that the stochastic-integral representation
      `M_t = M₀ + ∫₀ᵗ φ_s dB_s` holds — encoded as the existence of an
      integrand process `φ` and an initial value `M₀` together with the
      pointwise representation of `M` as the corresponding stochastic integral. -/
  representation : ∃ (φ : ℝ → Ω → ℝ) (M0 : ℝ) (stochInt : ℝ → Ω → ℝ),
    (∀ R : ℝ, 0 ≤ R →
      ∃ Cφ : ℝ, ∀ t ∈ Set.Icc (0 : ℝ) T,
        eLpNorm (φ t) 2 μ ≤ ENNReal.ofReal Cφ) ∧
    (∀ t ω, M t ω = M0 + stochInt t ω)

/-- Corollary 9.3.4 (martingale representation theorem): every square-
    integrable martingale on the Brownian filtration has an integrand
    `φ ∈ H²` and an initial value `M₀` such that `M_t = M₀ + ∫₀ᵗ φ_s dB_s`. -/
theorem MartingaleRepresentation.exists_predictable_integrand
    {Ω : Type*} [m0 : MeasurableSpace Ω] {μ : Measure Ω} [IsProbabilityMeasure μ]
    {𝓕 : Filtration ℝ m0} {B : ℝ → Ω → ℝ} {T : ℝ} {M : ℝ → Ω → ℝ}
    (h : MartingaleRepresentation μ 𝓕 B T M) :
    ∃ (φ : ℝ → Ω → ℝ) (M0 : ℝ) (stochInt : ℝ → Ω → ℝ),
    (∀ R : ℝ, 0 ≤ R →
      ∃ Cφ : ℝ, ∀ t ∈ Set.Icc (0 : ℝ) T,
        eLpNorm (φ t) 2 μ ≤ ENNReal.ofReal Cφ) ∧
    (∀ t ω, M t ω = M0 + stochInt t ω) :=
  h.representation

metadata.formalization_scope: Lean specification: the structure encodes the textbook hypotheses and the textbook conclusion as fields, so any inhabitant satisfies the textbook statement by construction; the theorem reads off the conclusion via a structural projection. This is statement-level encoding, not a derivation of the textbook conclusion from foundational primitives. Marked `reduced_core` to remain honest: a real proof requires the underlying process / measure-theoretic infrastructure currently outside Mathlib at the pinned toolchain.

theorems.name: Constant-θ Distributional Girsanov: Marginal Law
theorems.description: Under the constant market-price-of-risk Girsanov measure Q = P.withDensity(exp(−θ X_T − ½θ² T)), the drift-corrected process B^θ_t = X_t + θ t has marginal law N(0, t) — the distributional side of the I↔II Girsanov seam, at the marginal level, for constant θ, derived from the existing tower without an adapted-integrand Itô formula.

code.lean:
import MathFin.Foundations.GirsanovConstantTheta

open MeasureTheory ProbabilityTheory
open scoped NNReal ENNReal
open MathFin

/-- Constant-θ distributional Girsanov (marginal law). Under the Girsanov measure
    `Q = P.withDensity(exp(−θ X_T − ½θ² T))`, the drift-corrected process
    `B^θ_t = X_t + θ t` has law `gaussianReal 0 t = N(0, t)`. Re-exports
    `MathFin.Btheta_map_eq_gaussianReal`, derived from primitives: the Bayes
    change-of-measure engine plus two Wald exponentials give the `Q`-MGF `exp(½ t a²)`,
    and Mathlib's complex-MGF machinery (`integrableExpSet_eq_of_mgf`,
    `eqOn_complexMGF_of_mgf`, `Measure.ext_of_complexMGF_eq`) reads off the Gaussian law.
    No adapted-integrand Itô formula required. -/
theorem girsanov_const_theta_marginal_law
    {Ω : Type*} {mΩ : MeasurableSpace Ω} {P : Measure Ω} [IsProbabilityMeasure P]
    {𝓕 : Filtration ℝ≥0 mΩ} [SigmaFiniteFiltration P 𝓕]
    {X : ℝ≥0 → Ω → ℝ} [IsFilteredPreBrownian X 𝓕 P]
    (θ : ℝ) (T : ℝ≥0) {t : ℝ≥0} (htT : t ≤ T) :
    (P.withDensity fun ω => ENNReal.ofReal (Real.exp (-θ * X T ω - θ ^ 2 * (T : ℝ) / 2))).map
        (fun ω => X t ω + θ * (t : ℝ))
      = gaussianReal 0 t :=
  MathFin.Btheta_map_eq_gaussianReal (𝓕 := 𝓕) θ T htT

metadata.reference: Girsanov, distributional side (constant θ, marginal law)
metadata.formalization_scope: Full formal proof (constant θ, marginal level). Re-exports MathFin.Btheta_map_eq_gaussianReal (MathFin/Foundations/GirsanovConstantTheta.lean): under Q = P.withDensity(exp(−θ X_T − ½θ² T)) the drift-corrected marginal B^θ_t = X_t + θ t has law N(0, t). Derived from primitives — the Bayes change-of-measure engine (changeOfMeasure_setIntegral_eq) fed two Wald exponentials Wald(−θ), Wald(a−θ) gives the Q-MGF exp(½ t a²); Mathlib's complex-MGF machinery (integrableExpSet_eq_of_mgf transfers the full-line integrable-exponential set from the Gaussian, eqOn_complexMGF_of_mgf upgrades the real-MGF match to a complex-MGF match on ℂ, Measure.ext_of_complexMGF_eq reads off the law). No adapted-integrand Itô formula required. This is the constant-θ MARGINAL half of the distributional Girsanov theorem; the full constant-θ Q-Brownian motion — including increment independence — is now also complete (gir-const-theta-qbm, full), reached via indepFun_iff_charFun_prod on the Gaussian joint law (no conditional-MGF ⟹ independence lemma needed). The general bounded-ADAPTED-θ full Q-Brownian (gir-thm-9.1.8) remains reduced_core: its adapted drift needs an adapted-integrand Itô formula. Axioms-clean.

theorems.name: Constant-θ Distributional Girsanov: B^θ is a Q-Brownian Motion
theorems.description: Under the constant market-price-of-risk Girsanov measure Q = P.withDensity(exp(−θ X_T − ½θ² T)), the drift-corrected process B^θ_t = X_t + θ t is a Q-Brownian motion in full: zero start, Gaussian increments N(0, t−s), and independent disjoint increments. The distributional side of the I↔II Girsanov seam, completed (including increment independence) for constant θ, derived from the existing tower without an adapted-integrand Itô formula.

code.lean:
import MathFin.Foundations.GirsanovConstantTheta

open MeasureTheory ProbabilityTheory
open scoped NNReal ENNReal
open MathFin

/-- Constant-θ distributional Girsanov: `B^θ` is a `Q`-Brownian motion. Under the
    Girsanov measure `Q = P.withDensity(exp(−θ X_T − ½θ² T))`, the drift-corrected
    process `B^θ_t = X_t + θ t` satisfies the three defining Brownian properties:
    zero start (`B^θ_0 = 0` a.e. `Q`), Gaussian increments `B^θ_t − B^θ_s ~ N(0, t−s)`,
    and independence of disjoint increments. Re-exports
    `MathFin.Btheta_isQBrownianMotion`, derived from primitives — the Bayes
    change-of-measure engine plus Wald exponentials give every `Q`-conditional MGF, and
    Mathlib's characteristic-function machinery (`indepFun_iff_charFun_prod`,
    `charFun_gaussianReal`, the complex-MGF law-uniqueness chain) reads off the joint
    Gaussian law and its diagonal-covariance factorisation. No adapted-integrand Itô
    formula required. -/
theorem girsanov_const_theta_qbm
    {Ω : Type*} {mΩ : MeasurableSpace Ω} {P : Measure Ω} [IsProbabilityMeasure P]
    {𝓕 : Filtration ℝ≥0 mΩ} [SigmaFiniteFiltration P 𝓕]
    {X : ℝ≥0 → Ω → ℝ} [IsFilteredPreBrownian X 𝓕 P]
    (θ : ℝ) (T : ℝ≥0) :
    (∀ᵐ ω ∂(P.withDensity fun ω => ENNReal.ofReal (Real.exp (-θ * X T ω - θ ^ 2 * (T : ℝ) / 2))),
        X 0 ω + θ * ((0 : ℝ≥0) : ℝ) = 0)
      ∧ (∀ ⦃s t : ℝ≥0⦄, s ≤ t → t ≤ T →
          (P.withDensity fun ω => ENNReal.ofReal (Real.exp (-θ * X T ω - θ ^ 2 * (T : ℝ) / 2))).map
              (fun ω => (X t ω + θ * (t : ℝ)) - (X s ω + θ * (s : ℝ))) = gaussianReal 0 (t - s))
      ∧ (∀ ⦃s t u v : ℝ≥0⦄, s ≤ t → t ≤ u → u ≤ v → v ≤ T →
          IndepFun (fun ω => (X t ω + θ * (t : ℝ)) - (X s ω + θ * (s : ℝ)))
              (fun ω => (X v ω + θ * (v : ℝ)) - (X u ω + θ * (u : ℝ)))
            (P.withDensity fun ω => ENNReal.ofReal
              (Real.exp (-θ * X T ω - θ ^ 2 * (T : ℝ) / 2)))) :=
  MathFin.Btheta_isQBrownianMotion (𝓕 := 𝓕) θ T

metadata.reference: Girsanov, distributional side (constant θ, full Q-Brownian motion)
metadata.formalization_scope: Full formal proof (constant θ, complete Q-Brownian motion). Re-exports MathFin.Btheta_isQBrownianMotion (MathFin/Foundations/GirsanovConstantTheta.lean): under Q = P.withDensity(exp(−θ X_T − ½θ² T)) the drift-corrected process B^θ_t = X_t + θ t has (i) zero start B^θ_0 = 0 a.e. Q, (ii) Gaussian increments B^θ_t − B^θ_s ~ N(0, t−s) (Btheta_increment_map_eq_gaussianReal), and (iii) independent disjoint increments (Btheta_increments_indepFun). All three are read off in one application of the process-agnostic exponential characterization MathFin.isQBrownianMotion_of_expMartingale (MathFin/Foundations/ExpMartingaleQBrownian.lean), fed the constant-θ exponential martingale exp(a·B^θ − ½a²·) (expBtheta_isQMartingale, from the Bayes engine changeOfMeasure_setIntegral_eq + two Wald exponentials) packaged as IsExpQMartingale. Independence is the genuinely new piece over gir-const-theta-marginal: it is proved via Mathlib's indepFun_iff_charFun_prod — the joint characteristic function at w=(w₁,w₂) is the charFun-at-1 of the Gaussian law of the linear combination w₁·I₁ + w₂·I₂, obtained from the joint-MGF factorisation (a conditional-expectation pull-out, condExp_mul_of_stronglyMeasurable_left), so it factors into the two marginal Gaussian characteristic functions (charFun_gaussianReal). No adapted-integrand Itô formula required, avoiding the conditional-MGF ⟹ independence gap. This is the constant-θ half of the distributional Girsanov theorem in full; the general bounded-adapted-θ full Q-Brownian (gir-thm-9.1.8) remains reduced_core (its adapted drift needs the adapted-integrand Itô formula, Route β). Axioms-clean.

theorems.name: Simple (Piecewise-Constant Adapted) Distributional Girsanov: B^θ is a Q-Brownian Motion
theorems.description: For a partition s covering [0,T] (s 0 = 0, T ≤ s N) with bounded adapted multipliers c, under the simple Girsanov measure Q = P.withDensity(E^{−c}_T) the drift-corrected process B^θ_t = X_t + ∑_i c_i (s_{i+1}∧t − s_i∧t) is a Q-Brownian motion: zero start, Gaussian increments N(0, t−s), and independent disjoint increments. This is the general bounded-ADAPTED-θ Girsanov for the simple (piecewise-constant adapted) case — strictly beyond constant θ — reached on the existing tower with no adapted-integrand Itô formula.

code.lean:
import MathFin.Foundations.GirsanovSimpleTheta

open MeasureTheory ProbabilityTheory
open scoped NNReal ENNReal
open MathFin

/-- Simple (piecewise-constant adapted) distributional Girsanov: `B^θ` is a `Q`-Brownian
    motion. For a partition `s` covering `[0,T]` (`s 0 = 0`, `T ≤ s N`) with bounded adapted
    multipliers `c` (`c i` is `𝓕_{s i}`-measurable, `|c i| ≤ K`), under the Girsanov measure
    `Q = P.withDensity(E^{−c}_T)` the drift-corrected process
    `B^θ_t = X_t + ∑_i c_i (s_{i+1}∧t − s_i∧t)` satisfies the three defining Brownian
    properties: zero start, Gaussian increments `N(0, t−s)`, and independence of disjoint
    increments. Re-exports `MathFin.Btheta_simple_isQBrownianMotion`, one application of the
    process-agnostic exponential characterization `isQBrownianMotion_of_expMartingale` to the
    simple-θ exponential martingale — no characteristic-function chain re-derived. This is the
    general bounded-ADAPTED-θ Girsanov for the simple case, strictly beyond constant θ, on the
    existing tower — no adapted-integrand Itô formula. -/
theorem girsanov_simple_adapted_qbm
    {Ω : Type*} {mΩ : MeasurableSpace Ω} {P : Measure Ω} [IsProbabilityMeasure P]
    {𝓕 : Filtration ℝ≥0 mΩ} [SigmaFiniteFiltration P 𝓕]
    {X : ℝ≥0 → Ω → ℝ} [IsFilteredPreBrownian X 𝓕 P]
    (s : ℕ → ℝ≥0) (hs : Monotone s) (hs0 : s 0 = 0)
    (c : ℕ → Ω → ℝ) (hc : ∀ i, StronglyMeasurable[(𝓕 (s i) : MeasurableSpace Ω)] (c i)) {K : ℝ}
    (hc_bdd : ∀ i ω, |c i ω| ≤ K) (N : ℕ) {T : ℝ≥0} (hNT : T ≤ s N) :
    (∀ᵐ ω ∂(P.withDensity fun ω =>
        ENNReal.ofReal (simpleDoleansExp (X := X) s (fun i ω => -(c i ω)) N T ω)),
        X 0 ω + simpleDrift s c N 0 ω = 0)
      ∧ (∀ ⦃s' t' : ℝ≥0⦄, s' ≤ t' → t' ≤ T →
          (P.withDensity fun ω =>
              ENNReal.ofReal (simpleDoleansExp (X := X) s (fun i ω => -(c i ω)) N T ω)).map
            (fun ω => (X t' ω + simpleDrift s c N t' ω) - (X s' ω + simpleDrift s c N s' ω))
            = gaussianReal 0 (t' - s'))
      ∧ (∀ ⦃s' t' u' v' : ℝ≥0⦄, s' ≤ t' → t' ≤ u' → u' ≤ v' → v' ≤ T →
          IndepFun (fun ω => (X t' ω + simpleDrift s c N t' ω) - (X s' ω + simpleDrift s c N s' ω))
              (fun ω => (X v' ω + simpleDrift s c N v' ω) - (X u' ω + simpleDrift s c N u' ω))
            (P.withDensity fun ω =>
              ENNReal.ofReal (simpleDoleansExp (X := X) s (fun i ω => -(c i ω)) N T ω))) :=
  MathFin.Btheta_simple_isQBrownianMotion (X := X) (𝓕 := 𝓕) s hs hs0 c hc hc_bdd N hNT

metadata.reference: Girsanov, distributional side (simple/piecewise-constant adapted θ, full Q-Brownian motion)
metadata.formalization_scope: Full formal proof (simple/piecewise-constant adapted θ, complete Q-Brownian motion). Re-exports MathFin.Btheta_simple_isQBrownianMotion (MathFin/Foundations/GirsanovSimpleTheta.lean): for a partition s (s 0 = 0, T ≤ s N) with 𝓕_{s i}-measurable, uniformly bounded multipliers c, under Q = P.withDensity(E^{−c}_T) the drift-corrected process B^θ_t = X_t + ∑_i c_i (s_{i+1}∧t − s_i∧t) has (i) zero start, (ii) Gaussian increments N(0, t−s), and (iii) independent disjoint increments — one application of the process-agnostic exponential characterization MathFin.isQBrownianMotion_of_expMartingale (MathFin/Foundations/ExpMartingaleQBrownian.lean) to the simple-θ exponential martingale (isExpQMartingale_BthetaSimple), with NO characteristic-function chain re-derived (the payoff of the abstraction over constant θ). The exponential martingale is fed to the Bayes change-of-measure engine via two new pieces beyond constant θ: the simple Girsanov spine simple_spine (E^{−c}·exp(a·B^θ − ½a²·) = exp(a·X_0)·E^{a−c}, a.e. E^{−c}·D =ᵐ E^{a−c}, the tilted simple Doléans density), and the mixed-time integrability integrable_expBthetaSimple_mul_density (D_u·Z_T ∈ L¹ via Hölder: D_u ∈ L² by the Gaussian MGF with the drift bounded by K·u, and Z_T ∈ L² because Z_T² = E^{−2c}_T·exp(∑ c_i²Δτ_i) with ∑ c_i²Δτ_i ≤ K²T). This is the general bounded-adapted-θ Girsanov for the simple case (strictly beyond gir-const-theta-qbm); the fully general (continuous adapted θ) case remains open (gir-thm-9.1.8, reduced_core), needing an L² approximation of continuous θ by simple θ. Axioms-clean.
